title: fagraea ceilanica
intro: Fagraea ceilanica, also known as the Fraser's frangipani or the Fraser's flower, is a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of Western Australia and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Fagraea ceilanica include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit potential anticancer properties. Fagraea ceilanica can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential interactions with other medications.
